title: find_first() with --trace causes C++ compiler error
body:
See `TEST_TRACE` in `t_cover_expr` in #5719 .  Without that I get:
```
In file included from Vt_cover_expr_trace__ALL.cpp:8:
Vt_cover_expr_trace___024root__DepSet_h16c88dcf__0__Slow.cpp: In function âvoid Vt_cover_expr_trace___024root___eval_initial__TOP(Vt_cover_expr_trace___024root*)â:
Vt_cover_expr_trace___024root__DepSet_h16c88dcf__0__Slow.cpp:20:94: error: cannot convert âVlQueue<unsigned int>â to âIDataâ {aka âunsigned intâ} in assignment
   20 |     vlSelfRef.t__DOT__unnamedblk2__DOT__qv = vlSelfRef.t__DOT__unnamedblk2__DOT__q.find_first(
      |                                              ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
      |                                                                                              |
      |                                                                                              VlQueue<unsigned int>
   21 |                                                                                 [&](
      |                                                                                 ~~~~          
   22 |                                                                                 IData/*31:0*/ item__DOT__index,
      |                                                                                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
   23 |                                                                                 IData/*31:0*/ item) {
      |                                                                                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
   24 |             return ((1U & (item & (item >> 1U))));
      |             ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~                                            
   25 |         }
      |         ~                                                                                     
   26 |     );
      |     ~                                                                                         
```

I get the error with or without `--coverage-expr`.  If desired I can add an unsup test.
